limitations: Records are tool-level staging evidence, not official university conclusions.
Do not infer named tool availability from generic AI policy language.
snapshotHash values in this staging file are SHA-256 hashes of the captured evidence snippets; canonical ingestion should replace them with full source snapshot hashes.
Official tool tables/directories are split one explicit row at a time; nested provider/model mentions inside platform descriptions are not promoted to allowed tool records by default.

reviewNotes.note: Firecrawl did not find a public NUS-wide named approved-tools directory. The public teaching policy references NUS approved AI tools without exposing the list, so seed tools were not inferred.
OpenAI ChatGPT and Claude are mentioned elsewhere as examples of LLMs, but the official availability statement applies to Copilot, Gemini and NotebookLM only.
ChatGPT, Claude, DeepSeek and Perplexity appear under additional tools, not in the recommended/protected ETH account group, so they were not marked allowed.

Stanford AI Playground mentions model providers, but the v2 extraction keeps the primary official tool as the Stanford-hosted platform and does not create separate allowed records for nested model families.
Harvard Google Gemini Pro and Microsoft Copilot Pro rows are omitted from canonical records because v1 canonical slugs do not yet model paid variants separately from the Basic products.
